claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start the assessment:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out about Child Benefit eligibility! I'll help you work through this step by step.

**First question: Do you have a child or children you're inquiring about, and if so, how old are they?**

This is important because Child Benefit is assessed on a per-child basis, and age is a key factor in determining eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old and lives with you.

Since Alex is 18 (which falls in the 16-19 age range), his eligibility will depend on whether he's in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently enrolled in approved education or training?**

This would include things like:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications)
- Approved vocational training programs

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in education. He's doing his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! A-levels at sixth form college would typically count as approved education.

Let me gather a bit more information about Alex's situation:

**Is Alex working at all? If so, how many hours per week does he work?**

This is important because there are rules about the amount of paid work a young person can do while still qualifying for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at all. He's just focused on his studies at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. So Alex is 18, doing A-levels, and not working.

Now I need to ask about your relationship to Alex and your living situation:

**Are you Alex's parent (or someone who is responsible for him), and does he live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm his parent and he lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to claim Child Benefit here?**

This typically means you're:
- A UK resident, or
- Have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or
- Have refugee status or certain other immigration statuses that allow you to claim benefits

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester actually. I'm a UK resident, so no issues there.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to check an important aspect about who is responsible for Alex:

**Is anyone else also claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is there anyone else who could be considered to have equal or greater responsibility for him than you do?**

For example, does Alex spend time living with another parent or guardian, or is anyone else providing for his day-to-day care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming for him. He lives with me full-time and I'm responsible for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just confirm one final detail:

**You mentioned Alex is doing his A-levels. Do you happen to know when he started this course, or roughly when he is expected to finish?**

This helps ensure he's within the timeframe for approved education under the Child Benefit rules.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He started the A-levels in September last year, so he's in his first year. He should finish them in about a year and a half - summer 2026.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for all that information. Let me review everything you've told me about your situation with Alex.

Based on what you've shared:
- **Alex** is 18 years old
- He's in approved full-time non-advanced education (A-levels at sixth form college)
- He started in September last year and will finish in summer 2026
- He's not working
- He lives with you full-time
- You are his parent and responsible for him
- You live in the UK (Manchester) and are a UK resident
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

According to the UK Child Benefit rules:

**You ARE eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Young people aged 16-19 can qualify for Child Benefit if they're in approved education or training
- A-levels at sixth form college count as approved full-time non-advanced education
- Alex is not working (and certainly not working 24+ hours per week, which would disqualify him)
- You are his parent, he lives with you, and you're responsible for him
- You're a UK resident with the right to claim
- No one else is claiming for him

**What you should do next:**
You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. You'll need to provide Alex's birth certificate and your own identification. Since Alex is over 16, you'll also need to provide evidence of his education (such as a letter from his sixth form college confirming his enrollment).

Child Benefit will continue until Alex finishes his A-levels in summer 2026, or until he turns 20 (whichever comes first) - as long as he remains in approved education.

Do you have any other questions about your Child Benefit eligibility?
